title: Replace macOS-only md5 with portable hash function
description: All shell hooks, bin scripts, and uninstallers use 'printf ... | md5' which is macOS-only. On Linux, md5 doesn't exist (md5sum is the equivalent). Need a shared helper function that all scripts can source, falling back from md5 to md5sum. Affects: hooks/governor-hook.sh, hooks/session-start.sh, hooks/post-tool-use.sh, hooks/pre-compact.sh, hooks/user-prompt-submit.sh, bin/ctx, bin/governor, uninstall-ctx.sh, uninstall-governor.sh.
close_reason: Replaced all raw md5 calls with portable project_hash() from hooks/lib/hash.sh. Updated all hooks, bin scripts, install/uninstall scripts, and README.

title: bug: Unbounded _recent_tools list in Governor
description:
Every `evaluate()` call appends to `self._recent_tools` (in-memory list). In a long session with hundreds of tool calls, this list is never trimmed. Precondition checks scan the entire list each time, but only recent tools matter.
**Fix:** Cap `_recent_tools` to a configurable max length (e.g., 50 entries), dropping oldest entries on overflow.
---

title: bug: Default machine mismatch between governor.py and hooks
description:
`_build_governor()` in `governor/governor.py:569` defaults to `"machines.tdd_cycle.TDDCycle"` while all shell hooks (`governor-hook.sh`, `post-tool-use.sh`, `session-start-v2.sh`) default to `"machines.tdd.TDD"`. If `CTX_MACHINE` is unset and no `.machine` file exists, the CLI and hooks will use different machines.
**Fix:** Change the default in `_build_governor()` to `"machines.tdd.TDD"`.
---

title: Add automated tests for terminology gate
description: New untracked scripts in gates/terminology/ (check-terminology, scan-history, lib-terminology.sh) plus install-terminology-guard.sh and uninstall-terminology-guard.sh have no test coverage. Add tests/test_terminology_gate.py with subprocess-based integration tests covering: (1) check-terminology: no-blocklist passthrough, clean/dirty staged diffs, deleted lines not flagged, excludelist support; (2) scan-history: no-blocklist passthrough, clean history, file-content and commit-message violations; (3) installer: non-git-repo rejection, script installation, pre-commit hook creation, idempotency, appending to existing hook; (4) uninstaller: non-git-repo rejection, guard removal, empty hook deletion, missing hook graceful handling, script cleanup. Use HOME override pattern to isolate blocklist from real ~/.config/git/blocklist.txt.
close_reason: Added tests/test_terminology_gate.py with 22 integration tests covering check-terminology, scan-history, installer, and uninstaller. All 217 tests pass.

title: Add no-deep-nesting and no-loop-mutation ast-grep lint rules
description: Add two new ast-grep rules to enforce functional style: (1) no-deep-nesting — catches any for/if nested inside another for/if at depth 2+, stopping at function boundaries; (2) no-loop-mutation — catches mutation calls (append, extend, insert, pop, remove, add, discard, clear, update, setdefault, subscript assignment, augmented assignment, del) inside for loop bodies. Both rules go in scripts/lint/rules/. Tests follow existing pattern in test_lint_gate.py.
close_reason: Added no-deep-nesting and no-loop-mutation ast-grep rules with 16 tests. Both use stopBy function_definition to avoid false positives across nested defs.

title: chore: Resolve v1/v2 hook tension
description:
`user-prompt-submit.sh` (v1 keyword classification) runs alongside the v2 governor with no integration. Both inject context into the same prompt. With the governor active, keyword-based injection can be redundant or conflicting (e.g., injecting refactoring context while in `writing_tests`).
**Options:**
1. Disable v1 hooks when governor mode is active (check for `.machine` file)
2. Make v1 context injection subject to governor state filtering
3. Remove v1 entirely and migrate keyword classification into machine CONTEXT definitions
---
notes: Resolved: v1 (/ctx) and v2 (/governor) now use separate lock files. /ctx controls keyword-based injection via /tmp/ctx-locks/<hash>. /governor <machine> enables the governor via /tmp/ctx-governor/<hash>, /governor off disables it. All v2 hooks check the new lock path.

title: feature: Machine validation at import time
description:
Nothing prevents a machine from defining `BLOCKED_TOOLS` for a state that doesn't exist, or `AUTO_TRANSITIONS` referencing a nonexistent event. Silent misconfiguration leads to hard-to-debug runtime behavior.
**Proposal:** Add a `validate()` classmethod on `GovernedMachine` that checks:
- All states referenced in BLOCKED_TOOLS/CONTEXT/AUTO_TRANSITIONS exist
- All events referenced in SOFTNESS/PRECONDITIONS/AUTO_TRANSITIONS exist
- No cycles in AUTO_TRANSITIONS
- Run automatically on machine instantiation.
---

title: feature: Machine-driven session instructions
description:
`session-start-v2.sh` hardcodes TDD-specific workflow instructions (lines 42-73) regardless of which machine is active. If someone uses `FeatureDevelopment`, they still get "Start by writing a test."
**Proposal:** Add a `SESSION_INSTRUCTIONS` class attribute (or companion `.md` file path) to `GovernedMachine`. The session-start hook loads instructions from the active machine instead of hardcoding them.
---

title: feature: Configurable test framework detection patterns
description:
Both `post-tool-use.sh` and `governor.py`'s transcript scanner hardcode pytest-specific patterns (`FAILED`, `passed`, `python3 -m pytest`). This prevents use with `unittest`, `cargo test`, `go test`, etc.
**Proposal:** Let the machine define `TEST_PATTERNS`:
```python
TEST_PATTERNS = {
    "fail": [r"FAILED", r"ERROR collecting", r"\d+ failed"],
    "pass": [r"\d+ passed", r"OK \("],
    "command": [r"pytest", r"python3? -m pytest"],
}
```
Governor and hooks read patterns from the active machine. Determinism preserved -- patterns are static per machine, just not hardcoded in shell.
---

title: chore: pre-compact.sh should inject state-specific context
description:
`pre-compact.sh` reads the current state but only injects core context. It does not inject state-specific context from `CONTEXT[state]`. After compaction, the model loses state-specific guidance that was injected at transition time.
**Fix:** After injecting core context, also resolve and inject `machine.get_context(current_state)` files.
---
close_reason: Added 'context' CLI mode to governor that resolves state-specific context file paths. Updated pre-compact.sh to inject state-specific context files and machine workflow instructions alongside core context before compaction.

title: feature: Configurable governance bypass for always-allowed tools
description:
The governor hook bypasses `ctx-locks`/`ctx-state` commands via string matching. But non-destructive tools (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) are always allowed in every machine -- evaluating them adds latency for no benefit.
**Proposal:** Add `BYPASS_TOOLS` to `GovernedMachine` (or a governor config) listing tools that skip evaluation entirely. Default: `["Read", "Glob", "Grep", "Agent", "WebSearch", "WebFetch"]`.
---

title: feature: Audit trail rotation and cleanup
description:
`$CTX_AUDIT_DIR/<session_id>.jsonl` files grow forever. Long-running projects accumulate stale audit files with no cleanup mechanism.
**Proposal:** Add age-based cleanup in the session-start hook (delete audit files older than N days, configurable via `CTX_AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS`, default 30).
---

title: chore: Remove or implement SUB_MACHINES
description:
`FeatureDevelopment.SUB_MACHINES = {"implementing": "machines.tdd_cycle.TDDCycle"}` is defined but the governor has zero support for nested/hierarchical machines. It's dead code that implies a capability the system can't deliver.
**Options:**
1. Delete `SUB_MACHINES` from `FeatureDevelopment` and `GovernedMachine`
2. Implement nested machine support in the governor (significant effort)
---

title: feature: Configurable keyword classification
description:
`user-prompt-submit.sh` has keyword-to-file mappings hardcoded in shell. Projects can't customize which keywords trigger which context files without forking the hook.
**Proposal:** Move classification rules to a declarative config (e.g., `.claude/classification.json` or a machine attribute) that the hook reads at runtime.

title: chore: Transcript scanning performance
description:
`_check_transcript_for_pytest()` reads the entire JSONL transcript file on every PreToolUse hook invocation. As conversations grow (transcripts can reach 10k+ lines), this becomes increasingly expensive in the critical path of every tool call.
**Options:**
1. Read transcript from the end (seek to last N KB) since only the most recent Bash result matters
2. Cache the last-processed byte offset and only read new content
3. Store a processed-tool-use-ids set in the state file to avoid re-scanning
---
